channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.123
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.123 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: New AI gateway capabilities in Azure API Management
item.description: Multi-model, multi-protocol AI applications are quickly becoming the norm. Teams are mixing OpenAI, Anthropic, and Vertex AI models, exposing tools through MCP, and wiring agents together with A2A. As that surface grows, so does the work of keeping it secure, observable, and consistent. Our ongoing...
item.dc:creator: budzynski

item.title: Announcing Go support in Azure Functions (Preview)
item.description: We're excited to announce that Azure Functions now supports Go as a first-class language, available today in public preview on the Flex Consumption plan. Go developers can now build event-driven, serverless applications using idiomatic Go, the standard toolchain they already love, and the full...
item.dc:creator: AnthonyChu

item.title: Build, deploy, and govern sovereign AI with Foundry Local on Azure Local
item.description: Not every AI workload can run in the cloud. For many of our customers, data needs to stay within defined boundaries, connectivity may be limited or absent, and latency, governance, and auditability are non-negotiable. With Foundry Local on Azure Local, you can use the same model catalog, developer...
item.dc:creator: insagiv

item.title: Azure Connector Namespaces: managed integration for any Azure compute
item.description: The integration tax nobody budgets for It is always a simple task on paper: connect apps to the systems the business actually runs on — SharePoint, Salesforce, SAP, Outlook — and get back to building features. What gets in the way is rarely the business logic. It's the plumbing. You write a custom...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Announcing Preview of Guest RDMA for Azure Boost
item.description: We’re excited to announce Guest RDMA (Preview) in Azure, beginning today in our UKSouth region, bringing high-throughput, ultra-low latency networking directly into guest virtual machines anywhere within the same region. By using Azure Boost to enable RDMA capabilities on our frontend network...
item.dc:creator: MengxiWu

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Backup for Cosmos DB
item.description: You can now enable vaulted backups for Azure Cosmos DB accounts, protecting mission-critical data with secure, isolated, and resilient backups. Azure Backup for Cosmos DB, now in preview, supports cyber-resiliency and compliance needs, even in scenarios i
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: What’s new in Observability at Build 2026
item.description: Observability for AI and Agent Workloads AI agents are moving from prototype to production. To help teams ship and operate agents with the same rigor as the rest of their stack, Azure Monitor & Azure Copilot Observability agent brings end-to-end agent observability — grounded in OpenTelemetry so...
item.dc:creator: Priyanka Nanda

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Policy Coverage for Model Router in Foundry Models
item.description: Azure Policy now provides centralized governance for deployments using the model router. Organizations can define and enforce routing standards across their environment, helping ensure that model selection aligns with security, compliance, and operational
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Voice Live integration with Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service is now generally available for Voice Live integration, allowing developers to connect real-time speech-to-text and text-to-speech to a Foundry Agent without writing their own audio pipeline. Existing agent capabilities — in
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Announcing Azure Linux 4.0: Purpose-Built for Azure, Now in Public Preview
item.description: Today at Microsoft Build, we're announcing the public preview of Azure Linux 4.0 - Microsoft's first party Linux distribution, purpose-built for Azure. Azure Linux 4.0 is available now for Azure Virtual Machines, VM Scale Sets, and container images – with Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) support and...
item.dc:creator: poorvinarang

item.title: Introducing Azure Container Linux (ACL)
item.description: Today at Microsoft Build 2026, we’re announcing the general availability of Azure Container Linux (ACL): a secure, immutable container host designed to help platform teams run Kubernetes workloads at scale on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) with greater consistency, reduced operational overhead, and...
item.dc:creator: FloraTaagen

item.title: Closing the AI-readiness gap with agentic modernization
item.description: Legacy debt is widening the AI-readiness gap Legacy systems and mounting tech debt aren't just slowing your AI agenda — they're quietly stealing its potential. Aging architectures, and complex, decades-old applications, databases, and infrastructure weren't designed for high performance, complex,...
item.dc:creator: Mike_Hulme

item.title: Azure Functions at Build 2026 Update
item.description: Azure Functions took another big leap at Build 2026. It is now the best programming model for event-driven apps and agents, on the best infrastructure to write secure code that scales. The headline features: serverless agents, connectors to M365, Teams, and more, Go, MCP, and Durable Tasks....
item.dc:creator: nzthiago

item.title: 🎉 Automation just became a team sport. Meet Azure Logic Apps Automation.
item.description: Low barrier to entry. Built for production. Now in Public Preview There's a moment that plays out in almost every organization right now. Someone closest a business problem - a retail ops lead, a finance analyst, a security analyst looks at a repetitive process and thinks, this should just run...
item.dc:creator: DivSwa

item.title: 📢 Announcing Knowledge as a Service for Azure Logic Apps
item.description: Now in Public Preview Turn your documents into a ready-to-use knowledge base without custom RAG pipelines. Today at Microsoft Build 2026, we are announcing the Public Preview of Knowledge as a Service for Azure Logic Apps. It is a managed knowledge layer that transforms your documents into a...
item.dc:creator: DivSwa

item.title: Your first model deployment on Foundry Local on Azure Local: from catalog to inference in 10 minutes
item.description: Foundry Local on Azure Local is in public preview at the time of this writing. You've installed Foundry Local on your Azure Local cluster. The operator's pods are running, the CRDs are registered, you've checked it twice with kubectl get pods. Now what? This blog covers the part that comes right...
item.dc:creator: JonathanSchtechel

item.title: Azure DevOps and GitHub: Journeying into the AI Era
item.description: AI is changing how software gets planned, built, and reviewed. As teams adopt agentic development, the platform underneath those workflows matters more. They need tools that bring planning, coding, security, and collaboration together—and can keep pace with how development is evolving. That’s why...
item.dc:creator: Rajesh Ramamurthy

item.title: Better Together: Build Agents in Microsoft Foundry, Automate them with Azure Logic Apps
item.description: One agentic stack: Foundry brings the intelligence; Logic Apps brings the automation and orchestration. The promise of agentic AI is not simply more capable chat. It is autonomous work that advances real business processes. To achieve this, agents require two things: a place to be built with the...
item.dc:creator: DivSwa

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Sphere OS version 26.06 is now available
item.description: Azure Sphere OS version 26.06 is now available in the Retail feed. This release includes updates to Azure Sphere OS only; it does not include an updated SDK. If your devices are connected to the internet, they will receive the updated OS from the cloud.Th
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Automatic OS Image Upgrades for VMSS Flex
item.description: Automatic OS Image Upgrades for Virtual Machine Scale Sets (VMSS) using Flexible Orchestration are now in public preview, enabling consistent, fleet-wide OS updates with reduced manual effort. This capability extends automated image upgrades to VMSS Flex,
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: OpenTelemetry Metrics, Visualizations, and Enhanced Monitoring in Azure Monitor for Azure VMs and Arc Servers
item.description: OpenTelemetry metrics and visualizations in Azure Monitor are generally available, delivering an enhanced, unified monitoring experience for Azure Virtual Machines and Arc Servers. This release consolidates key monitoring capabilities into a single experi
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public preview: Azure Linux 4.0 for Azure Virtual Machines and VM Scale Sets
item.description: Azure Linux 4.0, Microsoft's first-party Linux distribution purpose-built for Azure, is now available in public preview for Azure Virtual Machines and VM Scale Sets. It brings a modernized stack — including Linux kernel 6.18 LTS, dnf5 package manager, gli
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Azure Container Linux (ACL) now generally available on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description: Azure Container Linux is a container-optimized, immutable operating system for AKS node pools — derived from Flatcar Container Linux and built on Azure Linux RPM packages. It delivers a locked-down, minimal host purpose-built for running containerized wor
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid - MQTT v5 Subscription Identifier
item.description: Azure Event Grid Standard Namespace now supports MQTT v5 subscription identifier capabilities in GA. Now each MQTT subscription can include an identifier that is delivered with incoming messages, enabling applications to route events immediately based on
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In development] Private Preview: multiparty analytics with Azure Confidential Clean Rooms
item.description: Microsoft is announcing the preview of multiparty analytics with Azure Confidential Clean Rooms, resulting in fully managed service that allows customers and their partners to securely analyze privacy sensitive datasets with Apache Spark based big-data an
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In development] Private Preview: Storage optimized Lasv5 and Laosv5 Azure VM series
item.description: The Lasv5 and Laosv5 storage optimized VMs, based on the 5th Gen AMD EPYC™ processor (Turin), are in preview. Lasv5-series is designed for storage-intensive workloads requiring high disk capacity, throughput, and I/O, while Laosv5-series is well-suited fo
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Github Copilot modernization
item.description: GitHub Copilot modernization agent is now generally available Coordinated orchestration from estate to code. Scale application assessments and upgrades across your entire app portfolio with GitHub Copilot modernization agent (GA), connecti
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Rolling updates in Flex Consumption
item.description: Rolling updates are now generally available in the Flex Consumption plan, delivering zero-downtime deployments with a simple configuration change.Instead of forcefully restarting all instances during code or configuration updates, the platform gracefully
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Dynamic threshold for Log search alerts
item.description: We’re excited to announce the General Availability of Dynamic threshold for Log search alerts! Using dynamic thresholds means you don't have to know the right threshold for each Log alert rule - Dynamic thresholds calculate the most appropriate thresholds
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Introducing the Azure Functions serverless agents runtime (preview)
item.description: We're thrilled to announce the Azure Functions serverless agents runtime, now in public preview. It brings a new, markdown-first programming model for building AI agents as a first-class workload on Azure Functions, with the event-driven triggers, scale-to-zero economics, and operational...
item.dc:creator: AnthonyChu

item.title: Azure Functions MCP Extension: What's New at Build 2026
item.description: The Azure Functions MCP extension has had a breakout year! Since its initial preview, the extension has grown from a single trigger type into a full-featured platform for building remote MCP servers: with tool, resource, and prompt triggers across multiple languages, MCP Apps for interactive UIs,...
item.dc:creator: lily-ma

item.title: Introducing On-demand Sandboxes for Azure Durable Task Scheduler (Private Preview)
item.description: Maybe it needs a native toolchain. Maybe it runs untrusted customer or LLM-generated code. Maybe it needs Python from a .NET orchestrator, or bursty compute that should scale to zero when the work is done. Today, we're thrilled to announce On-demand Sandboxes for Azure Durable Task Scheduler, now...
item.dc:creator: greenie-msft

item.title: Announcing Anyscale on Azure public preview: Powered by Ray on AKS
item.description: What's new The defining workload of the cloud era is no longer a web service or a database. It is a distributed AI training or inference job that needs thousands of GPUs, a coherent data plane, and a way for a handful of engineers to operate all of it without losing their weekends. Getting there is...
item.dc:creator: Brendan Burns

item.title: Announcing managed connectors for Azure Functions (Preview)
item.description: Azure Functions now includes managed connectors in public preview - the same 1,400+ connector ecosystem behind Logic Apps and Power Platform - as first-class triggers in your Functions code, plus typed SDKs for invoking connector actions from your function body. Build event-driven integrations with...
item.dc:creator: nzthiago

item.title: Introducing Azure Container Apps Sandboxes: Secure Infrastructure for Agentic Workloads
item.description: Today we are announcing the public preview of Azure Container Apps Sandboxes - a new first-class resource type that gives you fast, secure, ephemeral compute environments with built-in suspend and resume. This is the underlying infrastructure on which products like Cloud sandboxes in GitHub...
item.dc:creator: vyomnagrani

item.title: What's new in Azure Kubernetes Service at Microsoft Build 2026
item.description: A year ago, teams were still asking whether Kubernetes belonged anywhere near their AI workloads. That question has mostly been answered as the community comes together to . The real challenge now is operational: how to run training and inference at scale when cost, latency, and reliability are...
item.dc:creator: coryskimming

item.title: Hosted MCP Servers in Connector Namespace (Preview)
item.description: Imagine you've built an agent and you want to give it access to tools via MCP servers. Local servers won't work because your agent can't connect to them in production. Wouldn't it be great if you could quickly stand up secure, enterprise-ready remote MCP servers that your agent can use? This is...
item.dc:creator: lily-ma

item.title: What's new in Azure Container Apps at Build'26
item.description: Azure Container Apps (ACA) is a fully managed serverless container platform that enables developers to build and deploy microservices and modern applications without requiring container expertise or needing infrastructure management. ACA provides built-in autoscaling (including scale to zero),...
item.dc:creator: vyomnagrani

item.title: What's new in Azure API Management at Microsoft Build 2026
item.description: For more than a decade, Azure API Management has helped organizations secure, govern, and operate APIs at enterprise scale. As organizations build more AI-powered applications, APIs are increasingly part of a broader architecture that includes AI models, agents, MCP tools, and agent-to-agent...
item.dc:creator: beenamore

item.title: What's new in Azure Logic Apps at Microsoft Build 2026
item.description: Automation is entering a new era Automation has traditionally been powerful, but not always accessible. Building production-grade automations often requires specialized expertise in workflows, integrations, APIs, infrastructure, identity, security, networking, and operations. Organizations could...
item.dc:creator: beenamore

item.title: Azure Monitor Copilot Observability Agent: What’s new at Build
item.description: The Observability agent in Azure Copilot is an AI-powered assistant built into Azure Monitor that helps engineers investigate issues and explore their systems using natural language. By grounding its analysis in telemetry data such as metrics, logs, and traces, it supports both open-ended...
item.dc:creator: EfratNauerman

item.title: Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro is Generally Available
item.description: As organizations apply AI to real-world operations—from weather forecasting and infrastructure resilience to supply chains and environmental monitoring—geospatial data is becoming a critical enterprise input. GeoAI, or geospatial AI, applies deep learning, computer vision, and generative AI to...
item.dc:creator: Yves-Pitsch

item.title: Any source. Any destination. Ready for AI-era.
item.description: Telemetry is exploding, every new app, edge node, and AI agent is a new firehose, and AI has raised the bar on what that telemetry must be: governed, on open standards, observable at agent scale. Today, most teams answer that by stitching together a stack of disconnected tools, each catering to a...
item.dc:creator: shayoni_seth

item.title: Unlock On-Prem Productivity with Agentic Retrieval in Foundry Local
item.description: In today’s connected world, customers expect instant, context-rich interactions, even in environments where cloud connectivity isn’t guaranteed. That’s where Retrieval-Augmented Generation at the edge comes in. Since we launched into public preview, we’ve watched teams across regulated,...
item.dc:creator: moran_assaf

item.title: Introducing azure-functions-skills: An AI-Era Workspace for Azure Functions (Preview)
item.description: azure-functions-skills gives GitHub Copilot CLI, Claude Code, Codex CLI, and VS Code the skills, MCP configuration, hooks, and instructions needed to create, diagnose, deploy, and validate Azure Functions projects end-to-end. The post Introducing azure-functions-skills: An AI-Era Workspace for...
item.dc:creator: Tsuyoshi Ushio

item.title: Make any agent enterprise-ready with the Agent 365 SDK
item.description: One of the biggest barriers to enterprise adoption is the lack of centralized controls. Before deploying an agent broadly, organizations need clear answers: What is this agent allowed to do? What data can it access? How is it monitored? And how do we step in when something goes wrong? Today,...
item.dc:creator: aaronbjork

item.title: Scale On-Prem AI with Foundry Local on Azure Local: Multi-Node Inference and vLLM Support
item.description: Since announcing the public preview of Foundry Local on Azure Local for single-node, we’ve seen strong adoption in regulated industries and consistent customer demand to expand the platform for scalable deployments. Today, we’re expanding Foundry Local model offering on Azure Local (preview) with...
item.dc:creator: liranlyabock_microsoft

item.title: Announcing Preview of new Azure Lasv5 and Laosv5 VMs based on the AMD EPYC™ ‘Turin’ processor
item.description: We’re excited to announce the preview of the new Azure Lasv5 and Laosv5 Local Storage Optimized Virtual Machines (VMs) powered by the 5th Generation AMD EPYC™ (Turin) processors. Lasv5-series is designed for storage-intensive workloads requiring high disk capacity, throughput, and I/O, while...
item.dc:creator: sarah-zhou

item.title: Announcing the Public Preview of Integrated Embeddings in Azure Cosmos DB: Build AI Apps With Embeddings That Stay in Sync
item.description: AI applications built on Azure Cosmos DB depend on embeddings for grounded results. Keeping them in sync with your data is the hard part: it means building and operating a separate data pipeline to track changes, call an embedding model, and write the results back to Azure Cosmos DB. In practice,...
item.dc:creator: Abhishek Gupta
